comment.body:
Nobody will be banned for telling these ppl to 'fuck off'. Simply bcs it is understandable. But those ppl will be banned, bcs sorry, but I want to take care of ppl who came here to learn, to discuss constructively and to trade, and not to look for their personal financial advisor.
If you have no basic knowledge (even what your brokerage asks for, when you register your account... probably answered via chatGPT), thats your problem, not mine. 
If you cannot think in distributions (meaning, if this then A, if that then B... with different probabilities), then shut your mouth at least before you start whinning about your lack of understanding of my post, and ask proper and constructive questions

comment.body:
I can't tolerate this anymore. 
When I launched this substack, i was so happy with the community we got here. Discussions were constructive, and interesting. 
I came everyday to talk, bcs I felt like I wanna talk about things, markets, dynamics, a lot of times.. literally after every post-close. 
But for today, I don't even have the stomach to open the chat. I just send the mornign messages, that you either read or don't, and let the chat run.

But, man, I'm the freaking admin... 
I must take care of those ppl who stay away of the chat bcs of these persons. They private texting me and literally saying "it is mental", "disgusting", "gives me brain damage", etc.

comment.body:
47% of my subs are from the States.
And while I like the country and the american ppl, and i don't like its pop-culture at all.
What I mean by this, is basically exactly what the movie Dumb Money is all about. 
Ppl are advised to trade... it is very easy to do in the States... big promises, fake gurus on the TV keep shouting in your face... salesmen everywhere who want only one thing: to make you consume as much as you can... and if you spent all your money, borrow money from the bank. 
And if your debt burden is high, borrow more money from the banks to pay your debt back.
etc-etc.

comment.body:
in reality, a trader defines the distribution of the events. 
with different probabilities. 
understands then underlying mechanism, what causes them, and by that he can pre-project what can happen if A and if B, and if C...
different probabilities, not 50-50... 
and sizes accordingly.
He will lose on position A but win on position B
and if he assumed that A probability was lower than B, he will end up green.
Not become a millionaire, but will earn money.

Then cut the loser and size up the winner bet.

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
there are times when this is very obvious that A, B or C event will happen.
never 100%, but more than 90%

but if the analyst paints you A, B, C, D, etc. scenarios, you understand it and size your strategy on that. (And yes one of them will eventually happen... and no, it's not that "the analyst is right anyway"... thats a bad US mainstream media culture, to be the best, the always-right, the smartest kid in the room, etc. a very bad mentality. be a grown man for god's sake)
or if you don't understand the situation, stay in cash. Never trade what you don't understand.
Start little, start with easy patterns... and trade only what you understand. Trading is not a guessing race. You don't need to pass any expectations. It is to generate money for living. Nothing more.
